content:
German language strikes again, sorry for the wall of text. How one addresses the other is based on familiarity/situation and I can't pull that fully from English, since everyone uses a simple "you" there.

The confusion stems from me not wanting to take too many creative liberties when something else might've been intended. Or I'm just overthinking

E.g. 
Keeper addresses Hameln by name directly (informal),
Hameln replies with Mr./Miss %player% (formal)

This would read a bit as if the Keeper were either:
- way older than Hameln (old person old)
- his senior (power hierarchy)
- indicating closeness while Hameln is keeping his distance

Addressing someone informally, and being addressed back formally is seen in e.g. school (teacher vs student), and even then in late high school it switches to mutual formal. So it's not something done between two adults because it could be a tad disrespectful? At work we all use mutual informal because it is company policy. 

I could try to use my head and base it off the relationships the Keeper has, but I'd hate to misrepresent anything. 

I'd love to hear how other languages with this issue solved this, or any and all thoughts.
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: Unfortunately this is a common issue that has no elegant solution, it's something a lot of languages struggle with since honorifics often don't have a direct equivalent in other languages
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: it happens with translations of a lot of east asian languages (JP, KR, CN all have honorifics), though Chinese honorifics aren't quite as structured as the other two
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: there's still quite a few of them though, and unfortunately I think you may just have to make your own judgement depending on the context as that's about all you can do
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: Thank you! We shall just wing it as a team then
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content:
KR keeper here, at least in Korea I think formal-to-informal situations can mostly be summarized to 
1) teacher-student relationship, like you said
Or 2) 'when the younger/lower in power hierarchy isn't very intimate with the superior'. 

At first glance, the relationship between the Keeper and the Awakeners might seem like superior and subordinate. But our Keeper is far from such formalities, and would genuinely want to be more like friends to the Awakeners.
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: In characters, honorifics can be used to show that this character is polite/modest/a person of dignity.
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content:
I think the reasons some Awakeners address the keeper formally(based on JP dub) are:

1) They somewhat consider the Keeper as their master(Karen, Ogier, sometimes Lily)

2) showing religious modesty and virtues(Miriyam)
thread_title: A question regarding familiarity between Keeper and Awakeners

content: As for Hameln, I think it could be either to show he's polite and kinda classy, but maybe it could be related to the fact that (chapter8-9 content) ||the Keeper is a fragment of  Azathoth, and Hameln is based on Tru'nembra||.
